input.claim: A broad AI supply-chain basket across semis, memory, optics, data centers, and energy contains the next tenbagger candidates.
evidence_summary: NVIDIA results support continued AI data-center infrastructure demand.
Micron results support strong AI/data-center memory demand.
Roundhill Memory ETF packages exposure to global memory companies and began trading in April 2026.
SEC investor resources warn investors not to make investment decisions based solely on social-media stock recommendations.

risk_notes: Sector taxonomy is useful for research routing, but tenbagger prediction is not verifiable.
This should be used as a risk-review and router example, not as positive citation training.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Roundhill launched DRAM as a pure memory ETF in April 2026, bundling memory names such as Micron, SK hynix, Samsung, SanDisk, Western Digital, and Seagate; social posts also cite large YTD gains.
evidence_summary: Roundhill states DRAM began trading on April 2, 2026 and is a memory-focused ETF.
Roundhill lists memory-related holdings including SK hynix and SanDisk on the DRAM ETF page.
MarketWatch describes the Roundhill Memory ETF as recently launched and notes rapid gains and volatility in the memory-chip sector.

risk_notes: ETF facts are supported by the issuer.
Performance claims should be validated with point-in-time market data before use.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: AI compute hardware bottlenecks are moving into low-loss materials, orthogonal backplanes, glass substrates, and CPO; this creates a structural investment window.
evidence_summary: TSMC describes CoWoS-S as packaging for ultra-high-performance computing, AI, and supercomputing with HBM cubes and large interposers.
TSMC annual-report material says it is developing CoWoS, InFO, SoIC, and COUPE to enable large-scale interconnectivity at lower power.
Intel says glass substrates are part of advanced packaging innovation toward large transistor-count packages by 2030.
Sivers/GF collaboration supports optical connectivity architectures including CPO and LPO for data-center interconnects.

risk_notes: Technology stack exists, but 'structural squeeze' and 'golden investment window' require capacity, pricing, customer, and valuation evidence.
Use as deep-research task, not as direct investment recommendation.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Hyperscaler capex has risen sharply and may pressure free cash flow; a social post cites a Nomura-style chart claiming FCF for AMZN/MSFT/GOOG/META/ORCL is nearing zero while S&P 500 is at highs.
evidence_summary: Alphabet expected 2026 capex in the $175B-$185B range and said investments are for AI compute, Cloud demand, Services, and Other Bets.
Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125B-$145B, citing higher component pricing and data-center costs.
ProCap says hyperscaler capex is projected around $660B in 2026 and argues capex now approximates operating cash flow for the group.

risk_notes: Official sources support very large AI capex, but the exact “FCF near zero” chart requires original Nomura/filing calculation.
This is a good deep-research trace because it separates primary capex facts from secondary FCF interpretation.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Morgan Stanley was early to call a storage supercycle and remains a persistent NVIDIA bull; a newer report also highlights MLCC.
evidence_summary: Micron official results support strong memory demand and operating momentum.
Micron remarks state DRAM and NAND demand significantly exceeds supply.
KLA describes AI-chip manufacturing as requiring advanced process control across memory, logic, and packaging.

risk_notes: The Morgan Stanley timing and MLCC report claims require the actual sell-side reports or licensed summaries.
Public company evidence can support the memory cycle, but not the source-history claim.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: A YouTube creator named Ethan's U.S. stock notes has professional research background and publishes weekly U.S. stock reports that may be useful.
evidence_summary: No auditable source in this pilot verifies the creator's credentials, weekly-report quality, or citation discipline.

risk_notes: Do not import creator biography claims as fact without channel/about-page and sample-report evidence.
Good candidate for a source-quality audit workflow, not citation-verifier training yet.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Goldman Sachs reports called storage and MLCC shortages after large price moves, and these reports are useful for paid clients.
evidence_summary: Micron says DRAM and NAND demand significantly exceeds supply.
Micron's record results are consistent with strong memory-cycle fundamentals.
KLA explains AI chips require advanced process control across memory and packaging.

risk_notes: Goldman report timing and MLCC-specific calls require the actual proprietary report or a licensed auditable summary.
Public evidence supports memory tightness, not Goldman attribution.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: The ZhuLinsen daily_stock_analysis GitHub project is an AI-driven A/HK/US/JP/KR stock watchlist analysis system that can produce daily dashboards and push reports.
evidence_summary: The repository describes an AI-driven stock analysis system for A-shares, Hong Kong, U.S., Japanese, and Korean stocks.
The English README says the system analyzes watchlists daily, generates a decision dashboard, and pushes to Telegram/Discord/Slack/Email/WeChat Work/Feishu.
The project site says DSA integrates market data, news sentiment, technicals, fundamentals, and LLMs for individual-stock reports and market recaps.

risk_notes: Good source-discovery example for Kiwi's tool radar.
Do not treat the tool's generated investment reports as ground truth; they would need their own verification traces.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language
